ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists for the complete kaya toast breakfast set. FOODON_03315671 (toast food product) is used as the closest structural anchor for the toast component. The full set including kaya spread, salted butter, and coffee has no direct FoodOn representation.
ontology.foodex2_description: Bread, toasted — used as the closest FoodEx2 anchor for the toast base of this Singaporean breakfast set
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for kaya toast set or Singaporean kopitiam breakfast sets. A0CKP (toasted bread) is the closest defensible anchor for the primary solid component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical concept maps to kaya toast breakfast set as a food entity.

main_ingredients: kaya (coconut jam with eggs and sugar)
coffee (kopi O — black, or kopi/kopi C — with evaporated or condensed milk)
allergens: dairy (butter; kopi C contains evaporated milk)

serving.description: 1 set meal (2 slices of kaya butter toast + 1 cup of coffee)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is a set of two slices of toasted white bread spread with kaya and salted butter, accompanied by one cup of hot black coffee (kopi O) or hot white coffee (kopi or kopi C with evaporated milk), totalling approximately 300 g.

nutrient_source.title: Health Promotion Board Singapore — Nutrient Composition of Food (HPB Foodo / Energy and Nutrient Composition of Food)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single HPB entry exists for the complete Sinpopo kaya toast set. Values are estimated by component-weighted averaging using HPB Foodo entries for: (1) white bread toasted, (2) kaya (coconut jam), (3) salted butter, and (4) black coffee / white coffee with evaporated milk, proportioned to a 300 g set meal. The input record's per-100g values (energy 150 kcal, protein 3.33 g, fat 8.33 g, carbohydrate 15.0 g, sugar 8.33 g, sodium 133.33 mg, dietary fibre 0.67 g) are consistent with this component-weighted estimate and are retained. Additional micronutrients are sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for the individual components where HPB data is incomplete.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database — white bread analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Sinpopo kaya toast set. GI of 70 is assigned based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white bread (GI ~70, high category), which constitutes the dominant carbohydrate vehicle in this set. Kaya (coconut jam) adds sugar that does not lower the overall GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (70 × 45 g carbohydrate in 300 g serving) / 100 = 31.5. Available carbohydrate per serving = 15.0 g/100g × 300 g = 45 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Elevated sodium (133 mg/100g), saturated fat (5 g/100g) from salted butter and coconut-based kaya, and added sugar with low fibre (0.67 g/100g) and negligible potassium — all unfavourable for blood pressure management.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit this set to occasional consumption, request unsalted butter and reduced-sugar kaya, and choose kopi O (black coffee without condensed milk) to avoid additional sodium and sugar load from the white coffee variant.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~70) from refined white bread combined with 8.33 g sugar/100g from kaya and low dietary fibre (0.67 g/100g) produces rapid postprandial glucose elevation; a 300 g serving delivers a glycemic load of approximately 31.5.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should substitute wholemeal or multigrain bread to lower the GI, request reduced-sugar kaya, choose kopi O (unsweetened black coffee) over kopi C (with condensed milk), and limit portion size to one slice to reduce the glycemic load per meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 5 g/100g (from salted butter and coconut fat in kaya) and trans fat at 0.17 g/100g contribute to LDL-raising lipid load; the set also provides 13.33 mg cholesterol/100g and 133 mg sodium/100g with minimal protective fibre or potassium.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this set to occasional consumption, replace salted butter with a plant-based spread lower in saturated fat, choose kopi O over kopi C to avoid condensed milk, and pair with a fibre-rich side to partially attenuate postprandial lipid and glucose responses.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kaya Toast with Salted Butter and Coffee Set is a quintessential Singaporean kopitiam (coffee shop) breakfast, widely served across Singapore's hawker centres, coffee shops, and café chains such as Sinpopo, Ya Kun Kaya Toast, and Killiney Kopitiam. The set consists of two slices of thin white bread that are toasted until golden and crisp, then generously spread with kaya — a fragrant, sweet coconut-and-egg jam flavoured with pandan leaves — and a thick slab of cold salted butter. The contrast between the warm, crisp toast, the sweet aromatic kaya, and the cold salty butter is the defining sensory experience of this dish. The set is served with a choice of kopi O (hot black coffee brewed from dark-roasted Robusta beans, sweetened with sugar) or kopi/kopi C (hot white coffee with evaporated or condensed milk). Per 100 g of the combined set, the food provides approximately 150 kcal, 3.33 g protein, 8.33 g fat (of which 5 g is saturated), 15 g carbohydrate, 8.33 g sugar, 0.67 g dietary fibre, and 133 mg sodium. A standard 300 g serving delivers approximately 450 kcal and a glycemic load of ~31.5, classifying it as a high-glycemic meal. Clinically, the set is of concern for individuals with type 2 diabetes (high GI white bread, high sugar kaya, low fibre), hypertension (sodium from salted butter, added sugar, negligible potassium), and cardiovascular disease (saturated fat from butter and coconut kaya, trans fat, low fibre). The dish is vegetarian and generally halal. It is a culturally significant breakfast item representing the Hainanese immigrant coffee-shop tradition in Singapore and is recognised as part of Singapore's hawker food heritage.
common_user_queries: How many calories in kaya toast set?
What is the GI of kaya toast?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for the complete Sinpopo kaya toast set. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted averaging using HPB Foodo and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for individual components (white bread toasted, kaya, salted butter, black/white coffee). The input record's per-100g values are consistent with this estimate and are retained. GI is an analogous estimate based on white bread GI from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for the composite kaya toast set (including kaya's coconut fat fraction and butter's fatty acid profile combined with coffee) is not available as a single entry in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024. 2. potassium_mg is set to -1: potassium data for the composite set is not reported as a single entry in HPB Foodo; individual component potassium values from USDA SR Legacy could be used for a future refined estimate. 3. water_g is set to -1: moisture content for the composite set is not available in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central as a single entry. 4. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are set to -1: micronutrient data for this composite set is not available as a single entry in HPB Foodo; individual component data from USDA SR Legacy could support a future refined estimate. 5. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed negligible/zero per HPB Foodo and USDA SR Legacy for the components of this set (toasted white bread, kaya, butter, coffee); no vitamin-C-containing ingredients are present in material quantity. 6. The coffee component introduces variability: kopi O (black, sweetened) vs kopi C (with evaporated milk) will alter sugar, fat, and sodium values; the stated per-100g values represent a blended average of the two options as presented in the menu item. 7. trans_fat_g of 0.17 g/100g is retained from the input record; this is plausible given the salted butter component (dairy-derived trans fat). 8. Halal status is assessed as likely halal based on ingredients (no pork, no alcohol); formal halal certification for Sinpopo Coffee - Funan has not been independently verified in this record.
